AI just moved out of the cloud and into your actual computer. Not a chatbot, not a tool that answers questions, and leaves you to do the work. An autonomous agent that opens your files, runs your browser, executes your workflows, and delivers finished tasks while you focus on everything else. The new Manis update dropped something called Manis Desktop and a feature called My Computer. And I genuinely think this is one of the most significant shifts in AI we have seen this year. Most AI tools make you smarter. This one makes you faster. There is a difference. You stop being the person doing the work and start being the person delegating it to a machine that never stops. Right now, I am going to break down exactly what this update does, why it matters, and how you can start using it to run your business more efficiently. Today, stay with me because the examples I am about to show you are going to completely change how you think about what AI can actually do. Hey, if we have not met already, I am the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he is out there helping clients get more leads and customers, I am here to keep you up to date with the latest in AI. Julian reads every single comment. So, make sure you drop one below and let us know what you think about this update. Right, let us get into it. Here is something worth saying out loud. Most AI tools have a ceiling. You ask a question, you get an answer. Maybe it is a great answer, but then you close the tab and you go do the work yourself. The AI helped you think. It did not help you execute. And for anyone running a business, a content operation or a team, that ceiling gets frustrating fast. You do not need more ideas. You need more done. That is the gap Manis is built to fill. It is not positioned as a better chatbot. It is positioned as an autonomous agent, a system that takes a goal and completes it end to end without you babysitting every step. That is a fundamentally different product category and it is worth understanding why that matters. Manus is an autonomous AI agent designed to execute real world tasks rather than just generate responses. You give it a goal and it plans, performs, and completes the entire workflow. Think of it less like a tool and more like an AI employee. You delegate a task and it handles the execution from start to finish. It can build websites, research markets, analyze data, write and deploy code, automate browser tasks, and generate full reports. The reason this works is because Manis uses multiple agents operating together. Planner agent breaks down the goal into steps. Execution agent does the actual work, browsing, coding, file management. My knowledge agent finds and processes relevant information. Verification agent checks output quality before handing it back. These agents coordinate like a small team. They pass tasks between each other and self-correct as they go. That is why it can handle complex multi-step workflows that a single AI model would struggle to complete reliably. Now, here is where this update becomes genuinely interesting. Manus just launched Manis desktop and the headline feature inside it is called my computer. Before this update, Manis ran entirely in the cloud. Your tasks were being processed on external servers. That worked but it had limits. Now the agent lives on your actual machine, your computer, your local files, your apps. Manus can now read documents on your desktop, organize folders and manage local files, run terminal commands, automate workflows directly on your device, interact with your browser, coding environment, and productivity tools. The architecture shift is from cloud execution to local execution. And what that means practically is speed, privacy, and real integration with the software you already use every day. Your computer just became the thing the agent operates. That is a big deal. Quick pause here. We cover exactly how to implement tools like Manis, build systems around them, and use AI to save time and grow faster. No fluff, just what works. Link is in the description. Go check it out and come back. Let me give you practical examples so this clicks properly. Imagine you want to run a competitive analysis for the AI profit boardroom. You open Manis and you type something like, "Research the top 20 AI automation communities online. Find their content strategy, their main offers, and their positioning. Then build me a full report. Manis does not just Google it. It opens your browser, navigates to pages, pulls relevant data, organizes it into a structured document, and hands it back to you finished. Another example, you tell Manis, build a new landing page for the AI profit boardroom that converts, explains the value of AI automation, targets business owners looking to grow and drives signups. It will research competitor pages, write the copy, design a layout, generate the code, and give you a deployable page. A full web workflow completed autonomously. These are not hypothetical use cases. This is what the system is built to do. Use case understanding your market and positioning. Prompt to use. Research the top 20 AI automation communities online. Find their content strategy, main offers, and positioning. Then build me a full competitive analysis report with a summary table and key takeaways. I am what Manis does step by step to one opens browser and searches for AI automation communities. Two, navigates to each competitor's website and community page. Three, extracts pricing, content types, and audience positioning. Four, organizes findings into a structured comparison. Five, writes a summary with key insights and gaps in the market. Six, delivers a finished report document to your desktop. 4 to 6 hours of manual research done autonomously. Use case. Creating a high converting page for the AI profit boardroom. Prompt to use. Build a new landing page for the AI profit boardroom. Make it convert. Explain the benefits of joining an AI automation community. Target business owners who want to use AI to grow faster. Include a clear headline, benefit section, and signup CTA. Current what Manis does step by step. One, researches top performing landing pages in the online education and AI space. Two, identifies copy frameworks and headline formulas that convert. Three, writes full landing page copy, headline, sub headline, benefits, CTA 4, designs a layout structure with sections mapped out. Five, generates clean HTML CSS code for the page. Six, delivers a deployable file ready to go live. Time saved, full day design and copywriting project completed autonomously. There are two more features worth highlighting. The first is the Aldi browser operator. This lets Manis control websites on its own. It can navigate pages, fill in forms, extract data, and complete multi-step tasks online without you touching anything. The second feature is agent skills k. These are custom automation workflows you build once and reuse. Think of them as reusable AI automations for recurring tasks. You could build a skill that checks your top competitors every week, pulls their latest content, summarizes what is performing well, and delivers a report. set it up once and it runs on a repeat. That kind of leverage used to require a whole team to maintain. Two things before you go.
